Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:36 ET
Massive index put blocks clash with single-name call surge before tomorrow's CPI
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Institutions are loading defensive hedges through 150K SPY put blocks while betting on individual stock outperformance — classic dispersion setup before CPI.
  • 2. Positive dealer gamma at $7B creates artificial stability that could amplify moves if breached below SPY $667.
  • 3. Vol surface shows backwardation in 12 mega-caps signaling elevated near-term event risk pricing ahead of tomorrow's inflation print.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge before CPI

What stands out is the extreme structural divergence between index protection demand and single-name bullishness, with the P/C ratio split reaching 1.37x (index 2.24 vs single-name 0.87) — the widest defensive dispersion setup since August. Massive put blocks confirm this positioning: 150K SPY May 530 puts at $0.73 and dual 26K IWM put spreads, totaling $182M in index downside protection. This occurs as NVDA call blocks surge 78K contracts and crypto miners like HUT (+215% call flow) and RIOT (+194%) attract aggressive bullish positioning, suggesting institutions are hedging broad market risk while maintaining conviction in specific growth themes. The positive gamma environment at $7.1B in SPY creates deceptive stability that masks underlying tension, with the gamma flip sitting just 1% below spot at $667. Implied correlation at 0.10 confirms the stock-specific regime, making dispersion trades highly efficient as single-name volatility trades independently from index moves. Twelve names showing backwardation — including AAPL, META, and COIN — price elevated near-term risk ahead of tomorrow's CPI, while chemical stocks (LYB +927% puts, DOW +415%) suggest sector rotation fears. This setup intensifies with CPI landing in 24 hours and positive dealer gamma that could amplify any break below key support levels. The divergence between Millennium's aggressive AMZN reversal (398% add in Q4) and today's defensive index positioning suggests smart money expects volatility around the inflation print while maintaining selective equity exposure.

Layer 1: Sentiment +38Layer 2: GEX +$7.1BLayer 3: P/C split 1.37xLayer 9: CPI tomorrow
